headlines.title: James Comey indicted over seashell photo that officials say threatened Trump
headlines.description: The Department of Justice has secured an indictment for former FBI Director James Comey over a photo he posted on social media showing seashells arranged in the shape of "8647," which Trump allies portrayed as a threat to President Trump, according to two sources familiar with the matter.

headlines.title: King Charles emphasizes strength of U.S.-U.K. alliance in address to Congress
headlines.description: King Charles III addressed Congress on his historic trip to the U.S. The king called for the U.S. to help defend Ukraine and stressed the importance of the U.S.-U.K. alliance and NATO. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles and Monica Alba report on the remarks.

headlines.title: UAE announces it will leave OPEC
headlines.description: The United Arab Emirates said it would leave the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries after being a part of the group that coordinates oil output among the world’s top producers for over five decades. NBC News’ Christine Romans reports on the motivations behind the UAE’s withdrawal.

headlines.title: How the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner chaos unfolded
headlines.description: Secret Service faced challenges evacuating President, Vice President, Cabinet officials and Congressional leaders after gunman rushes D.C. gala.

headlines.title: FCC expected to order Disney stations to file broadcast license renewals early
headlines.description: The Federal Communications Commission is expected to order Disney's eight owned-and-operated television stations to file their broadcast license renewals ahead of schedule, according to a source with knowledge of the matter. NBC News’ Chole Melas details how the order is related to late-night host Jimmy Kimmel's comments about first lady Melania Trump.

headlines.title: White House condemns 'political violence' after correspondents' dinner shooting
headlines.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt began a press briefing by thanking the law enforcement response to the White House Correspondents' Association dinner and condemning "political violence" and rising rhetoric in the country.

headlines.title: Suspect Cole Tomas Allen charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump
headlines.description: The man accused of opening fire outside the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner was charged with three counts in his first court appearance, including attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump. Cole Tomas Allen also faces charges of interstate transportation of weapons and discharge of a firearm during a violent crime.

headlines.title: Elon Musk blasts Sam Altman over OpenAI restructuring in tech trial
headlines.description: Elon Musk testified in court that OpenAI's Sam Altman betrayed the organization's principles.

headlines.title: Cop haunted for decades by Roxanne Sharp's brutal killing didn’t live to see suspects arrested
headlines.description: For decades, former Louisiana police officer Herbert Joiner used a brown leather briefcase to store the records, clippings and clues he had collected about a case that had haunted him since 1982 — the brutal gang rape and murder and 16-year-old Roxanne Sharp.

headlines.title: Mullet Toss kicks off at Florida-Alabama state line
headlines.description: The 2026 Interstate Mullet Toss is underway at the Flora-Bama in Perdido Key where thousands gathered to see who could throw their fish the farthest across the Florida-Alabama state line. NBC News' George Solis reports.

headlines.title: King Charles calls for the U.S. to help defend Ukraine
headlines.description: During his address to a join meeting of Congress, King Charles made an explicit call for the U.S. to help fund defense for Ukraine, comparing the situation to NATO’s support of the U.S. following 9/11.

headlines.title: Justice Department indicts Comey over seashell photo
headlines.description: Justice Department indicts Comey over seashell photo
